items.title: $500 fiber optic HDMI cable delivers flawless 48 Gbps performance across a staggering 990 feet — crushes 8K at 60 Hz and 4K at 120 Hz over long distances
items.description: The HDMI standard can often be unclear, with the same version number referring to completely different capabilities across products. You can technically pay your way out of this confusion by going with an expensive fiber-optic HDMI cable that maintains its full bandwidth over much longer distances. That's what this Twitter user did, and their cable has one more trick up its sleeve.

Subscribe to our newsletter Ultra-expensive cables positioned as a miracle cure have been around forever. Most of these are obviously shams, but some are actually really worth the price, such as those that use fiber optics. Cables ditching copper wires to improve signal integrity are not exactly rare, but they were a new discovery for this Twitter user, who got their hands on an $116 fiber-optic HDMI cable from Ruipro with detachable ends.Go deeper with TH Premium: GPUs (Image credit: Noctua)Desktop RoadmapEnterprise RoadmapRubin in-depthThe Stout Owl: The ultimate Noctua G2 PCThe X post below went viral, garnering almost a million views for what seems like a perfectly ordinary product. Be that as it may, most people don’t actually care for, or even know, tech specs off the top of their head, especially when it comes to confusing standards like HDMI. What we’re looking at here is an active optical cable (AOC), which carries its signal over fiber optics instead of traditional copper wiring.It’s called “active” because there’s a signal conversion taking place inside the cable, where electrical HDMI signals are converted into optical signals between the two ends. The cable isn’t entirely devoid of copper wires; some are still used for low-priority communication and power, but the majority of the signal is carried via fiber optic strands. This allows for significantly longer cables that can travel long distances without degradation.Article continues below You may like Start-up plans to use terahertz radio frequencies for communication between servers instead of copper or optical connections Brit installs adapters to convert telephone lines into gigabit ethernet carrier throughout his house Optical device beams data at speeds up to 25 Gbps via light, up to 25 kilometer range with ultra-low latency Look at this HDMI cable I just got 👀This isn’t a regular HDMI cable, it’s fiber optic. The signal is transmitted with light instead of copper, so there’s basically zero signal loss even over long distances.It’s HDMI 2.1, so it supports everything you’d want: 8K60, 4K120,… pic.twitter.com/T5ZnQOrf3CMarch 26, 2026Because this is an HDMI 2.1 cable in the post, it's capable of 8K output at 60 Hz, or 4K at 120 Hz without DSC, and supports 10-bit HDR. It's a full-fat 48 Gbps cable, which isn't always guaranteed, given how loose the HDMI Forum is with its branding. In fact, this is why people often buy overpriced cables: to circumvent the marketing red tape and get the best possible option that'll cover all their needs.Now, these specs aren't special in a vacuum, but the fact that the cable can enable them over (up to) 990 feet — that's the impressive bit. The "entry-level" $116 version is only 3 feet long, and for that, it's quite expensive because you don't need fiber optic for this length. The best deal here is probably the 100-foot cable priced at $150, so only about $30 more for an extra 97 feet of fiber-optic goodness.Ruipro has made the HDMI connectors on both ends removable, so you won't have to replace the entire cable if a plug breaks. When removed, the end of the cable can slot into keystone jacks and wall plates as well for easy storage. The cable itself is relatively thin for its size, and the connectors are made entirely of metal to ensure durability.Another benefit of fiber optic is its resistance to electromagnetic interference, though that's not a huge issue to begin with for HDMI, and EMI is notoriously used as the bait to sell those aforementioned miracle cures. Regardless, this is still a solid HDMI 2.1 cable for those who value signal integrity, and even though the starting price is certainly not enticing, the subsequent options are priced rather fairly.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.
items.author: Hassam Nasir
items.titleZh: 500美元光纤HDMI线材实现990英尺无损48Gbps传输——轻松胜任远距离8K 60Hz与4K 120Hz信号传输
items.descriptionZh:
近期，一条关于光纤HDMI线缆的推文引发了近百万次的观看，使得这类相对小众的高端线材进入大众视野。推文中展示的是Ruipro品牌一款售价116美元、端头可拆卸的光纤HDMI线缆。虽然市场上摒弃铜线以提升信号完整性的线缆并不罕见，但许多人对此类产品的具体技术原理和优势仍不甚了解，尤其是面对HDMI这类复杂的标准时。

这条线缆本质上是一种**有源光缆**（Active Optical Cable, AOC）。其核心创新在于，它并非完全使用铜线传输信号，而是在线缆内部进行了**光电转换**：输入端的电信号（HDMI信号）被转换为光信号，通过光纤进行传输，到达输出端后再转换回电信号。线缆内仍保留少量铜线，用于低优先级通信和供电，但主干信号通过光纤传输。这种设计带来了革命性的优势：**信号在极长距离内传输也不会衰减**。

性能方面，文中提到的线缆符合**HDMI 2.1标准**，提供完整的**48 Gbps带宽**，支持无需DSC（显示流压缩）的**8K@60Hz或4K@120Hz**输出，并兼容10位HDR。这些规格本身在高端铜缆上也能实现，但该光纤线缆的惊人之处在于其**传输距离可达990英尺（约302米）**，而同等长度的铜缆几乎无法保证无损传输。

价格策略上，入门级的3英尺（约0.9米）版本售价116美元，对于短距离应用而言性价比不高，因为铜缆完全足以胜任。但其长距离版本则显得颇具竞争力，例如100英尺（约30.5米）版本仅售150美元，仅比短款贵约34美元，却提供了近百英尺的光纤传输能力。

产品设计也有亮点：**两端的HDMI接头是可拆卸的**。这种设计不仅方便更换损坏的插头，还能将线缆端头插入墙面面板或配线架的Keystone模块中，便于理线和隐藏布线。线缆本体相对纤细，接头采用全金属材质以保证耐用性。

此外，光纤传输天生具备**抗电磁干扰（EMI）** 的能力，尽管对于HDMI应用环境而言，EMI通常不是主要问题，且常被一些“玄学”线材营销过度夸大。

总结来说，Ruipro的这款有源光纤HDMI线缆是为特定需求场景设计的专业解决方案：**它主要面向需要超长距离（例如超过15-20米）、且必须保证无损传输超高分辨率和高刷新率信号的专业影音安装、大型会场、数据中心或高端家庭影院项目**。对于普通用户的标准家用距离（1-3米），高端铜缆仍是更经济务实的选择。这款产品揭示了在高端影音和专业领域，通过光电转换技术突破铜缆物理限制的可行路径。

items.title: Intel Arrow Lake Refresh CPU prices shot up to 17% above MSRP just 48 hours after launch — dynamic pricing trumps manufacturer's recommended pricing
items.description: The new Core Ultra 200S Plus series has received widespread acclaim within the community, and we liked it as well for rejuvenating Intel's otherwise underwhelming CPU generation. Part of the reason they're getting good reviews is their excellent price-to-performance ratio, which seems to be slightly undone at the moment.

Subscribe to our newsletter Intel announced its Arrow Lake Refresh series earlier this month, with a March 26, 2026, launch for the Core Ultra 5 250K Plus and Core Ultra 7 270K Plus. As of this writing, it's been just two days since these CPUs were released, yet they're already selling above MSRP across retailers. The Core Ultra 7 270K Plus and the Core Ultra 5 250K Plus currently sit at around $349.99 and $219.99, respectively. Meanwhile, the iGPU-less Core Ultra 5 250KF Plus sells for $199.99.Check out the Core Ultra 5 250KF Plus on NeweggCheck out the Core Ultra 5 250K Plus on NeweggCheck out the Core Ultra 7 270K Plus on NeweggThe MSRP for the Core Ultra 7 270K Plus is $299, which means it's $50 more expensive than its list price on Newegg at the moment. We were able to find it for a bit less on B&H, but it's out of stock there. The Core Ultra 5 250K Plus was announced at $200, but it's selling for $220, while its iGPU-less counterpart, the Core Ultra 5 250KF Plus, is listed at $200, up $15 from its original ask of $185.Those prices are consistent across vendors, so getting a Core Ultra 200S series CPU at MSRP — even if the difference is minor — might not be possible right now. We're going through an unprecedented economic climate as the entire PC industry is stuck in limbo, navigating both geopolitical chokepoints and the AI boom's unending appetite for production lines.It's a borderline miracle we're even getting new value-oriented chips in this climate. We called the 250K Plus the best CPU for $200 in our review and gave it an Editor's Choice award. The 270K Plus is also a big improvement over its predecessor, but it falls behind when in gaming compared to X3D chips. Moreover, no matter how impressive these chips might be, the LGA 1851 socket is unfortunately a dead end.That might put some people off buying the Arrow Lake refresh, since Nova Lake is supposed to be a truly generational leap for Intel, perhaps even catapulting them ahead of AMD after years of stagnation. If you don't plan on upgrading and just want a good CPU now, the 250K Plus for $220 is a great deal, while the 270K Plus for $350 is another solid option for flagship performance without spending $500 on the 285K. items.author: Hassam Nasir
items.titleZh: 英特尔箭湖Refresh版CPU发布仅48小时后价格飙升至厂商建议零售价17%以上——动态定价机制凌驾于厂商指导价之上
items.descriptionZh:
英特尔于本月早些时候宣布了其Arrow Lake Refresh系列处理器，其中Core Ultra 5 250K Plus和Core Ultra 7 270K Plus已于2026年3月26日上市。令人关注的是，尽管发布仅两天，这两款CPU已在各大零售商处普遍出现溢价销售现象。

目前，Core Ultra 7 270K Plus的市场价格约为349.99美元，Core Ultra 5 250K Plus约为219.99美元。而无集成显卡的型号Core Ultra 5 250KF Plus售价为199.99美元。这与英特尔的官方建议零售价存在明显差距：Core Ultra 7 270K Plus的MSRP为299美元（现溢价约50美元），Core Ultra 5 250K Plus的MSRP为200美元（现溢价20美元），而Core Ultra 5 250KF Plus的MSRP为185美元（现溢价15美元）。该溢价趋势在各大供应商中表现一致，这意味着目前几乎无法以官方建议零售价购得Core Ultra 200S系列处理器，即便差价并不巨大。

这一市场状况发生在特殊的宏观经济背景下。整个PC产业正处在一个前所未有的、近乎停滞的“模糊期”，同时面临地缘政治导致的供应链瓶颈，以及AI热潮对半导体产能的持续争夺。在此环境下，英特尔仍能推出面向主流市场的换代芯片，实属不易。

从产品性能来看，Tom‘s Hardware在评测中将Core Ultra 5 250K Plus评为“200美元价位最佳CPU”并授予“编辑选择奖”。Core Ultra 7 270K Plus相比前代产品亦有显著提升，但在游戏性能方面仍落后于AMD的3D V-Cache（X3D）系列芯片。然而，一个重要的制约因素是，这些芯片所采用的LGA 1851插座平台已被确认是“终点站”，下一代Nova Lake架构将更换接口。

这可能会让部分消费者对购买Arrow Lake Refresh系列犹豫不决，因为预计Nova Lake将是英特尔一次真正的代际飞跃，甚至可能帮助其在多年的僵持后实现对AMD的领先。不过，如果用户并无近期升级计划，只是当前需要一款性能出色的CPU，那么220美元的Core Ultra 5 250K Plus性价比突出，而350美元的Core Ultra 7 270K Plus则为不愿花费500美元购买顶级型号Core Ultra 9 285K的用户提供了一个坚实的旗舰性能选择。

总而言之，Arrow Lake Refresh系列在严峻的市场环境中展现了其产品竞争力，但初期的溢价和即将换代的平台限制，是消费者在购买决策时需要权衡的关键因素。

items.title: Radeon RX 9070 XT bundle bonanza includes free SSDs, AIO coolers, and power supplies for $699 — Red Team rescues gamers with reasonably-priced GPU bundles
items.description: AMD’s RX 9070 XT drops to $699 with bundled extras, offering better overall value in a volatile market.

Subscribe to our newsletter The AMD Radeon RX 9070 XT is one of the best graphics cards in 2026. The graphics card is now available at $699 through different bundles. Newegg has listed the ASRock Challenger Radeon RX 9070 XT at $729.99, the Gigabyte Radeon RX 9070 XT Gaming OC at $739.99, and the Sapphire Pulse Radeon RX 9070 XT at $769.99. By applying dedicated promo codes at checkout, buyers can bring the price down to $699, plus a free copy of Crimson Desert and an additional PC component, depending on the model.Check out the Sapphire Pulse Radeon RX 9070 XT deal on NeweggCheck out the ASRock Challenger Radeon RX 9070 XT deal on NeweggCheck out the Gigabyte Radeon RX 9070 XT Gaming OC deal on NeweggThe ASRock Challenger Radeon RX 9070 XT includes a 360mm AIO liquid cooler, the Gigabyte Radeon RX 9070 XT Gaming OC comes with a free 512GB PCIe 4.0 SSD, and the Sapphire Pulse Radeon RX 9070 XT bundles a 750W Gold-rated power supply.The Radeon RX 9070 XT is a well-rounded 4K gaming GPU that offers performance on par with the RTX 5070 Ti at a lower cost. That said, the GPU market continues to spiral, as street prices for most modern GPUs are significantly higher than MSRP due to ongoing DRAM shortages driven by AI. On top of that, AMD recently raised the price of its Radeon RX 9000 series by $10 per 8GB of VRAM, effectively increasing the MSRP of the RX 9070 XT from $599 to $619.The Radeon RX 9070 XT was introduced last year, based on AMD’s RDNA 4 architecture, with 64 compute units, a boost clock speed of 2.97 GHz, 16GB of GDDR6 VRAM with 20 Gbps transfer speeds over a 256-bit interface, and a TBP (Total Board Power) of 304W. Our testing found that the RX 9070 XT delivers similar performance in both 1440p and 4K gaming at ultra settings compared to Nvidia’s RTX 5070 Ti. It is worth noting that the 5070 Ti is currently priced significantly higher, with prices starting at $1,000 compared to its MSRP of $749.For those looking to upgrade, these deals make the RX 9070 XT a more compelling option, especially with the free bundled items on Newegg. However, with prices still elevated across the board, buyers will need to weigh whether to jump in now or wait for the market to stabilize. items.titleZh: AMD RX 9070 XT显卡礼包狂潮：699美元即享免费固态硬盘、一体水冷和电源套装——红队以高性价比组合助力玩家
items.descriptionZh:
AMD Radeon RX 9070 XT 作为2026年顶尖显卡之一，现已通过多种捆绑套装上市，起售价为699美元。Newegg平台列出了多款非公版型号的具体售价：华擎 Challenger Radeon RX 9070 XT 售价729.99美元，技嘉 Radeon RX 9070 XT Gaming OC 售价739.99美元，蓝宝石 Pulse Radeon RX 9070 XT 售价769.99美元。消费者在结账时使用专属促销码，即可将价格降至699美元，并额外获赠《赤色沙漠》游戏副本以及一项随型号不同的PC组件——华擎型号捆绑360mm一体式水冷散热器，技嘉型号附赠512GB PCIe 4.0固态硬盘，蓝宝石型号则搭配750W金牌认证电源。

该显卡基于AMD RDNA 4架构打造，拥有64个计算单元，加速频率达2.97 GHz，配备16GB GDDR6显存，通过256位接口实现20 Gbps的传输速率，整板功耗（TBP）为304W。在性能表现上，RX 9070 XT 在1440p和4K分辨率下开启超高画质设置时，性能与英伟达 RTX 5070 Ti 持平，但当前市场售价显著低于对手。值得注意的是，RTX 5070 Ti 建议零售价为749美元，实际市价已飙升至1000美元起步，而RX 9070 XT 在捆绑优惠后性价比尤为突出。

然而，受人工智能热潮导致的DRAM持续短缺影响，整个GPU市场价格依然高企，多数现代显卡的实际售价远高于官方建议零售价。AMD近期亦实施显存关联调价策略，每8GB显存价格上涨10美元，致使RX 9070 XT 建议零售价从599美元上调至619美元。在当前市场环境下，有意升级显卡的用户需权衡：是趁捆绑优惠入手RX 9070 XT，还是等待市场价格趋于稳定。对于追求4K游戏体验且注重性价比的玩家而言，此次促销使RX 9070 XT 成为极具吸引力的选择。

items.title: Samsung preps PCIe 5.0 QLC SSD with a controller based on open-source RISC-V architecture — BM9K1 delivers speeds up to 11.4 GB/s for 'personal AI workloads'
items.description: The BM9K1 reportedly delivers sequential read speeds of up to 11.4 GB/s, which Samsung said is 1.6 times faster than its predecessor, the PCIe 4.0 BM9C1.

Subscribe to our newsletter As reported by BigGo Finance, Samsung has unveiled the company's next-generation BM9K1 SSD at the China Flash Market Summit 2026 (CFMS 2026). The BM9K1, which will rival the best SSDs, is a PCIe 5.0 drive with QLC NAND and features an SSD controller built around the open-source RISC-V architecture.Go deeper with TH Premium: AI shortages (Image credit: Nvidia)AI data centers are swallowing the world's memory and storage supplyChip scarcity assaults auto industry amid the worsening Nexperia and DRAM crisisSamsung and SK hynix shorten memory contracts as pricing power shifts back to suppliersMemory makers are set to earn $551 billion from the AI boomThe BM9K1 reportedly delivers sequential read speeds of up to 11.4 GB/s, which Samsung said is 1.6 times faster than its predecessor, the PCIe 4.0 BM9C1. Samsung is targeting a 2027 launch in 512GB, 1TB, and 2TB capacities and positioning the drive for what the company calls “personal AI computing” workloads on desktops and laptops.Notable with the BM9K1 is Samsung’s departure from an Arm-based controller to a proprietary one built in-house using the open-source RISC-V instruction set architecture. The company said the RISC-V design enables more granular firmware optimization for QLC NAND management and AI-specific I/O patterns, resulting in a 23% improvement in energy efficiency over the BM9C1. That gain in efficiency is naturally going to make a big difference in thermally constrained, small-form-factor applications focused on AI.Article continues below You may like Micron announces 3610 SSD, the industry-first PCIe 5.0 QLC SSD available to OEMs Chinese SSD maker YMTC lists its first commercial PCIe 5.0 SSD as worldwide shortage intensifies Phison's new SSD controller sips a mere 2.3W and runs at 14.7 GB/s, addressing PCIe 5.0's power concerns Samsung BM9K1 SSD SpecificationsSwipe to scroll horizontallyRow 0 - Cell 0 Samsung BM9K1Samsung BM9C1Micron 3610Samsung 9100 ProInterfacePCIe 5.0 (NVMe)PCIe 4.0 (NVMe)PCIe 5.0 (NVMe 2.0)2.0)PCIe 5.0 (NVMe)NANDQLCQLCQLC (G9 276-layer)TLC (V8)Seq. read11.4 GB/s5 GB/s11 GB/s14.8 GB/sSeq. writeNot disclosed4.5 GB/s9.3 GB/s13.4 GB/sControllerSamsung RISC-VSamsung 5nm-Samsung 5nm PrestoCapacities512GB, 1TB, 2TB2TB512GB, 1TB, 2TB1TB, 2TB, 4TB512GB, 1TB, 2TB, 4TB, 8TBWith its introduction, the BM9K1 also enters a small category of similar drives. Micron’s 3610, announced at CES back in January, is the only other PCIe 5.0 QLC client SSD announced so far. The 3610 reaches 11 GB/s sequential reads and 9.3 GB/s writes, and Micron is already sampling it to OEM partners in capacities up to 4TB. Samsung's drive edges ahead in raw sequential read throughput at 11.4 GB/s, but tops out at 2TB and has not disclosed write speeds.Both QLC drives sit well below Samsung's own TLC-based 9100 Pro and its OEM counterpart, the PM9E1, which delivers up to 14.8 GB/s of read speed using V8 TLC NAND and the 5nm Presto controller. There’s a cost trade-off, though, with QLC packing four bits per cell versus TLC's three, yielding higher density and lower per-gigabyte pricing at the expense of write endurance and sustained write performance.Samsung plans to bring the BM9K1 to market in 2027, but hasn’t disclosed any information on pricing or form factor. items.titleZh: 三星发布采用开源RISC-V架构控制器的PCIe 5.0 QLC固态硬盘——BM9K1型号为个人AI工作负载提供高达11.4 GB/s传输速度
items.descriptionZh:
在2026年中国闪存市场峰会（CFMS 2026）上，三星电子正式发布了其下一代消费级固态硬盘BM9K1。该产品旨在与市场上顶级SSD竞争，是一款采用PCIe 5.0接口和QLC NAND闪存的驱动器，其最大亮点在于搭载了基于开源RISC-V架构自主研发的SSD主控芯片。

**背景与产品定位**
随着AI数据中心消耗全球大量存储资源，以及芯片短缺持续影响多个行业，存储市场正经历快速变革。三星将BM9K1定位于满足台式机和笔记本电脑的“个人AI计算”工作负载，计划于2027年上市，提供512GB、1TB和2TB三种容量选择。

**核心技术原理与创新点**
BM9K1的核心创新在于其控制器架构的转变。三星放弃了此前采用的Arm架构，转向基于开源RISC-V指令集架构自研主控。这一转变使得三星能够进行更精细的固件优化，专门针对QLC NAND的管理和AI特有的I/O访问模式进行定制。QLC（四层单元）NAND通过在单个存储单元中存放4比特数据，实现了比传统TLC（三层单元）更高的存储密度和更低的每GB成本，但在写入寿命和持续写入性能上存在折衷。新的RISC-V控制器通过对QLC的优化管理，在能效上取得了显著提升。

**性能参数与对比分析**
据三星披露，BM9K1的顺序读取速度最高可达11.4 GB/s，这比其前代PCIe 4.0产品BM9C1（5 GB/s）快了1.6倍。能效方面，得益于RISC-V架构的优化，BM9K1比BM9C1提升了23%，这对于注重散热和功耗的紧凑型AI计算设备至关重要。

目前，市面上已宣布的PCIe 5.0 QLC消费级SSD仅有美光在年初CES上发布的3610型号。根据提供的对比表格，美光3610的顺序读写速度分别为11 GB/s和9.3 GB/s，且已向OEM伙伴提供最高4TB容量的样品。三星BM9K1在顺序读取速度上以11.4 GB/s略微领先，但最大容量为2TB，且未公布写入速度。

两款QLC产品的性能均显著低于三星自家采用TLC NAND和5nm Presto控制器的旗舰产品9100 Pro（及其OEM型号PM9E1），后者的读取速度高达14.8 GB/s。这清晰体现了QLC与TLC之间的性能/成本权衡：QLC以牺牲部分写入性能和耐用性为代价，换取了更高的存储密度和更低的单位成本。

**技术影响与应用场景**
BM9K1的发布标志着RISC-V架构在关键存储组件中实现了重要的商业化落地。其更高的能效优化特别适合应用于对热设计功耗（TDP）敏感的轻薄笔记本电脑、小型台式机以及专注于本地AI推理和内容创作的“个人AI计算”场景。在AI工作负载日益从云端向边缘设备扩散的趋势下，此类高性能、高能效的存储解决方案将变得愈发重要。

**总结**
三星BM9K1 SSD通过采用创新的自研RISC-V控制器和QLC NAND，在PCIe 5.0平台上实现了显著的性能提升与能效进步。尽管在绝对性能上与传统TLC旗舰产品有差距，且初期容量选择有限，但其针对AI工作负载和能效的优化设计，预示着存储产品正朝着更专业化、更适应边缘计算需求的方向发展。该产品预计2027年上市，具体定价和物理尺寸（如是否为标准M.2 2280）尚未公布。

items.title: Meta to fund seven new natural gas power plants to fuel AI data centers — Entergy partnership to deliver 7 gigawatts of power for Louisiana AI facility
items.description: Meta is paying for the construction of seven new natural gas plants to supply its largest data center, massively expanding the fossil fuel infrastructure underpinning its AI buildout.

Subscribe to our newsletter Meta is paying for the construction of seven new natural gas plants to supply its largest data center, massively expanding the fossil fuel infrastructure underpinning its AI buildout. The company reached a new agreement with Entergy Corp. to build the plants, which will provide an additional 5.2 gigawatts of electricity to the Hyperion campus in Richland Parish, Louisiana, Entergy's local subsidiary said Friday, as reported by Bloomberg.Entergy received approval to build three gas plants last year, which will generate roughly 2.3 gigawatts of electricity for Meta. With the new expansion, Hyperion will be served by a total of 10 natural gas facilities, delivering more than 7 gigawatts. Entergy received regulatory approval for that initial batch of plants in 2025 and subsequently applied to connect additional gas generation to Louisiana's grid to meet rising demand. Still, the seven new plants will need their own sign-off from state regulators before construction can begin.Meta is not just paying for power generation, though; the company will also fund 240 miles of new transmission lines connecting South Louisiana to North Louisiana and Arkansas, battery energy storage systems, and nuclear power uprates at existing Entergy facilities. The scope of this commitment will extend well beyond the plants, effectively underwriting a regional grid expansion to serve a single campus.Article continues below You may like Meta inks deals to supply a staggering 6 gigawatts in nuclear power for data center ambitions Data center developers building private natural gas 'Shadow Grid' power plants to sidestep strained grids Meta sets up 'Meta Compute' organization for gigawatt-scale AI data centers Go deeper with TH Premium: AI and data centers (Image credit: Microsoft)Photonics and high-speed data movement is the next big AI bottleneckThe data center cooling state of playMassive AI data center buildouts are squeezing energy suppliesUltra Ethernet: The data center interconnection of tomorrowThe deal is structured so that Meta “pays its full cost of service,” according to Entergy, which projects the agreement will deliver more than $2 billion in customer savings over 20 years. Meta declined to say how much it would spend on the gas plants and associated infrastructure, per Bloomberg. Of Hyperion's total power draw, approximately 5 gigawatts will go to compute workloads, with the remainder powering broader campus operations, a Meta spokesperson said.All this comes amid a growing political fight over who pays for AI-driven electricity demand. Earlier this month, President Donald Trump demanded that technology companies pledge to cover their own power costs, ensuring residential bills don’t rise as a result of the data center infrastructure boom.Rachel Peterson, Meta's vice president of data centers, says that the Entergy filing aligns with the so-called Ratepayer Protection Plans proposed by the White House and with Louisiana’s “business-friendly [regulatory] environment.” A Meta spokesperson said that the company remains committed to its climate goals, but didn’t elaborate on how building a total of ten gas plants to feed its AI ambitions fits within them.The agreement includes a Meta commitment to help fund up to 2.5GW of new renewable energy resources, and the two companies also signed a memorandum of understanding to explore the future development and use of nuclear power.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.
items.author: Luke James
items.titleZh: Meta投资兴建七座天然气发电厂为AI数据中心供能——Entergy合作项目将为路易斯安那州AI设施提供7吉瓦发电能力
items.descriptionZh:
Meta近日与其数据中心所在地的主要电力供应商Entergy公司达成一项重大协议，将投资建设七座新的天然气发电厂，以支持其位于路易斯安那州里奇兰教区的最大数据中心园区Hyperion的扩张。此举旨在满足其人工智能业务快速发展所带来的巨大电力需求，同时也引发了关于谁应为AI驱动的能源消耗增长买单，以及科技巨头气候承诺与化石燃料基础设施扩张之间如何平衡的广泛讨论。

根据协议，这七座新电厂将为Hyperion园区额外提供5.2吉瓦的电力。加上去年已获批准建设的三座可提供约2.3吉瓦电力的天然气电厂，未来为该园区供电的天然气设施总数将达到十座，总供电能力超过7吉瓦。其中，约5吉瓦的电力将直接用于AI计算负载，其余部分则用于支持园区的其他运营。

除了发电设施本身，Meta的投资范围还延伸至关键的电网基础设施。该公司将资助建设长达240英里（约386公里）的新输电线路，用以连接路易斯安那州南部与北部以及阿肯色州，同时还包括电池储能系统以及对Entergy现有核电站的升级扩容。这一系列投资实质上是在为一个单一的数据中心园区推动整个区域的电网扩张。

财务安排上，Entergy表示该协议构建为Meta“支付其全部服务成本”的模式，并预计这一合作将在未来20年为其他电力客户节省超过20亿美元的费用。尽管Meta未透露具体投资金额，但这一举措无疑规模巨大。

这一扩张计划正处于日益激烈的政治辩论背景下。近期，美国前总统特朗普曾要求科技公司承诺承担自身的电力成本，以确保居民电费不会因数据中心基础设施的激增而上涨。Meta数据中心副总裁Rachel Peterson对此回应称，与Entergy的协议符合白宫提出的“费率保护计划”精神，也与路易斯安那州“商业友好的监管环境”相一致。不过，Meta仍需就新建的七座电厂获得州监管机构的单独批准。

在环保承诺方面，Meta表示仍致力于其气候目标。作为协议的一部分，Meta承诺将协助资助建设高达2.5吉瓦的新可再生能源发电资源。此外，双方还签署了一份谅解备忘录，旨在探索未来核能的开发与利用，暗示了其长期能源战略的多元化方向。然而，建设总计十座天然气电厂来支持AI发展的做法，如何与其碳中和目标相协调，Meta并未给出详细说明。

总体而言，这项协议凸显了AI算力爆炸式增长对全球能源基础设施带来的空前压力。科技巨头为确保稳定、大规模的电力供应，正不得不直接介入并投资基础发电和输电网络的建设，这正在重塑能源行业的格局，并引发关于能源公平、成本分摊和环境保护的复杂议题。

items.title: Chinese universities performing military research acquired Super Micro servers with sanctioned Nvidia AI chips — public documents reveal purchases were completed in 2025 and 2026 despite US export controls
items.description: Public documents revealed that four Chinese universities, including two that worked on military research for the PLA, were able to acquire servers with Nvidia A100 AI chips despite the U.S.'s export controls.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 1 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Tom's Hardware Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware Newsletter Get Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox. You are now subscribed Your newsletter sign-up was successful An account already exists for this email address, please log in. Subscribe to our newsletter Public documents reveal that four Chinese universities, two of which are known for conducting military research for the People’s Liberation Army, bought AI servers containing export-controlled Nvidia chips from Super Micro Computer. According to Reuters, the Harbin Institute of Technology (HIT), which focuses on missile, satellite, and robotics technologies, bought a system from Super Micro in July 2025 that contained eight Nvidia A100 AI GPUs.Beihang University, which also conducts aerospace and defense research, is said to have acquired a machine-learning workstation from the company just this March, configured with four Nvidia A100 chips. These two universities are part of the “Seven Sons of National Defense,” a designation for Chinese academic institutions with deep ties to China’s Ministry of Industry and Information Technology and the People’s Republic of China's defense industry.Go deeper with TH Premium: AI and data centers (Image credit: Microsoft)Photonics and high-speed data movement is the next big AI bottleneckThe data center cooling state of playMassive AI data center buildouts are squeezing energy suppliesUltra Ethernet: The data center interconnection of tomorrowNvidia’s A100 chips are less powerful than H200 AI GPUs, which U.S. President Donald Trump has finally allowed for export to China in late 2025. However, potential buyers still need to acquire export licenses from the federal government before they can get their hands on these chips, and it’s unlikely that PLA-linked institutions will ever get approval.Article continues below You may like US Senators call for a halt to Nvidia GPU exports in the wake of the Super Micro scandal US senators want to suspend Nvidia AI chip export licenses to China and its intermediaries The Super Micro AI accelerator smuggling scandal proves how cut-throat the global AI race has become — as global trade evolves, so does export control evasion It’s unclear how the universities were able to purchase these Super Micro servers. However, three individuals, including Super Micro co-founder Yi-Shyan “Wally” Liaw, were recently arrested for smuggling $2.5 billion worth of advanced AI servers into China. The conspirators allegedly shipped orders from resellers and shell companies located in different countries in Southeast Asia. Once the servers containing the controlled AI GPUs arrived at the local warehouse, their serial numbers were transferred to dummy servers, and they were then issued new fake documentation. Once completed, they were forwarded to their final destination in China.News like this has several American lawmakers on edge, with U.S. Senators Jim Banks (R-Ind.) and Elizabeth Warren (D-Mass.) writing a letter to Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick urging him to suspend the issuance of export licenses to China and its intermediaries until the issue has been resolved. It would be a blow to Nvidia if the administration grants the request, especially as the company has finally received Chinese orders for its H200 chips after several months of uncertainty from Beijing. items.titleZh: 公开文件显示，中国从事军事研究的高校购入了配备英伟达制裁AI芯片的超微服务器——尽管美国实施出口管制，采购仍在2025至2026年完成。
items.descriptionZh:
根据汤姆硬件（Tom's Hardware）获取的公开文件及路透社报道，中国四所高校从美国服务器制造商超微公司（Super Micro Computer）购买了配备受出口管制英伟达人工智能芯片的AI服务器。其中两所高校——哈尔滨工业大学和北京航空航天大学——因与中国国防工业关系密切而备受关注。

**背景与上下文**
这两所大学均隶属于被称为“国防七子”的中国高校群体，这些机构与中国工业和信息化部及国防工业有着深厚联系。哈工大专注于导弹、卫星和机器人技术，而北航则主要从事航空航天与国防研究。报道显示，哈工大于2025年7月购买了一台搭载八颗英伟达A100 AI GPU的超微系统；北航则于2025年3月获得了一台配置四颗A100芯片的机器学习工作站。

**核心技术原理与创新点**
英伟达A100 GPU是基于Ampere架构的数据中心级AI加速器，采用7纳米制程，配备第三代Tensor Core，专为大规模AI训练和推理工作负载设计。其核心创新在于多实例GPU（MIG）技术，可将单颗GPU物理分割为多个独立实例，提升资源利用率；同时支持高速NVLink互连，实现多GPU间的高带宽通信。尽管其性能不及后续发布的H200（基于Hopper架构，配备HBM3e内存），但A100因其强大的双精度浮点计算能力和在AI领域的广泛应用，仍被美国商务部列入出口管制清单。

**性能参数与对比数据**
A100的峰值FP16 Tensor Core性能可达312 TFLOPS，配备40GB或80GB HBM2e内存，带宽达1.6TB/s至2TB/s。相比之下，H200在相同精度下的性能显著提升，并拥有141GB的HBM3e内存，带宽达4.8TB/s。值得注意的是，美国总统特朗普已于2025年底批准向中国出口H200，但潜在买家仍需获得联邦政府的出口许可证，且与中国人民解放军有关联的机构极难获得批准。

**事件细节与走私手法**
目前尚不清楚这些大学如何成功采购到这些服务器。但近期超微联合创始人廖益贤等三人因涉嫌向中国走私价值25亿美元的先进AI服务器被捕。据指控，共谋者通过位于东南亚不同国家的经销商和空壳公司下单。装有受控AI GPU的服务器运抵当地仓库后，其序列号被转移至“傀儡服务器”，并伪造新的文件。完成后，服务器被转运至中国的最终目的地。

**技术影响与应用场景**
此类高端AI芯片可应用于前沿科研领域，如自动驾驶模拟、流体动力学计算、气候建模以及航空航天器的数字孪生。在军事相关研究中，它们可能用于导弹轨迹优化、卫星图像智能分析或自主机器人系统的训练。尽管A100并非最新型号，但其计算能力仍远超许多商用GPU，对受制裁实体而言具有重要价值。

**政治反应与行业影响**
此事已引发美国议员担忧。参议员吉姆·班克斯和伊丽莎白·沃伦已致信商务部长，敦促在问题解决前暂停向中国及其中介机构发放出口许可证。若政府批准该请求，将对英伟达构成打击——该公司在经历数月来自中国订单的不确定性后，刚刚开始获得H200芯片的中国订单。这一事件也凸显了全球AI竞赛的激烈程度，以及随着国际贸易形态演变，规避出口管制的手段亦在不断升级。

整体而言，该事件揭示了高端AI芯片供应链的脆弱性，以及在复杂地缘政治背景下，技术管制与反管制之间的持续博弈。

items.title: Ambitious modder bolts a 360mm server AIO onto an RTX 3080, slashes VRAM temps in half — enormous workstation cooler powers 54 degree drop, 9% performance uplift
items.description: Trashbench decided to take an Arctic WS360 360mm server AIO cooler and strap it directly to a GeForce RTX 3080
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 2 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Tom's Hardware Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware Newsletter Get Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox. Contact me with news and offers from other Future brands Receive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors By submitting your information you agree to the Terms & Conditions and Privacy Policy and are aged 16 or over. You are now subscribed Your newsletter sign-up was successful An account already exists for this email address, please log in. Subscribe to our newsletter We love a beautifully unhinged cooling mod, and Australian overclocker and YouTuber Trashbench is arguably the champion of the genre. We've previously covered his exploits, like leveraging a camping chiller to aggressively overclock an RTX 5050, but in this latest video, Trashbench decided to take an Arctic WS360 360mm server AIO cooler and strap it directly to a GeForce RTX 3080. The result? A massive, aesthetically pleasing mod that halves the card's VRAM temperatures and offers impressive efficiency gains.See, the Arctic WS360 is built for massive workstation CPUs. That means its copper cold plate is more than twice the size of a standard consumer liquid cooler. Surprisingly, this massive footprint makes it almost ideal for a modern GPU, as the cold plate is large enough to completely cover both the GPU core and the surrounding GDDR6X memory modules, slotting almost perfectly between the rows of VRM components. Once installed, it looks remarkably stylish, almost as if it were engineered specifically for the 3080. A pile of 3D printed brackets created in his attempt to fabricate a functional mounting system for the huge WS360 liquid cooler on the RTX 3080. (Image credit: Trashbench)Actually getting it to fit, however, was another story. Because the WS360's cold plate is so large, it completely obscures the standard GPU mounting holes. Trashbench shows off the project with his signature brand of Aussie nonchalance, but beneath the laid-back presentation, it's clear this took serious effort, requiring a dozen or more trial-and-error iterations to design and 3D-print a custom, functional mounting system.Article continues below You may like GPU cooler gets stripped to run ice cold water in its heatpipes, modded RTX 2060 benchmarks at 13 °C 100W car amplifier hacked into a custom RTX 3080 heatsink Modder pushes rare 20GB RTX 3080 Ti past 550W with risky power shunts and liquid metal cooling Once the server cooler was successfully seated, the thermal results in 3DMark Time Spy were excellent, if not exactly shocking for a cooler of this pedigree. You can see the screenshot below, but for the sake of those with screen readers, his GPU core temperature dropped by 26°C, his GPU core hotspot temperature dropped by 39°C, and the VRAM temperature plummeted by 54°C, dropping from a scorching 104°C down to a frosty 50°C. The gains from applying the massive liquid cooler were stark. (Image credit: Trashbench)Because modern GPUs are highly sensitive to thermal limits, this massive drop in temperatures yielded immediate performance dividends. Without even applying a manual overclock, the liquid-cooled RTX 3080 matched the performance of the air-cooled card pushed to its absolute maximum OC. Even better is that the water-cooled card consumed less power at stock settings than the air-cooled version, while simultaneously delivering superior performance.When Trashbench applied a manual overclock to the liquid-chilled card, he was able to squeeze out an additional +195 MHz on the core clocks. That translates to an average gaming performance uplift of around 9% over the stock air-cooled baseline. Not world-shattering perhaps, but NVIDIA doesn't give GPU tuners a lot of headroom for overclocking these days, so this is about as good as it gets without BIOS or shunt mods. I Put a Server Cooler on an RTX 3080 - YouTube Watch On Now, as always with extreme mods, we don't recommend trying to replicate this at home. The Arctic WS360 is an expensive piece of server hardware; Trashbench noted his was supplied by Arctic. Dialing in the 3D-printed mounting brackets took a massive amount of patience, too. Still, it serves as a brilliant demonstration of just how much performance and efficiency headroom modern GPUs are hiding behind their thermal limits.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.

items.titleZh: 硬核玩家为RTX 3080加装360毫米服务器水冷，显存温度骤降一半——巨型工作站散热器实现54度温降与9%性能提升
items.descriptionZh:
澳大利亚超频玩家兼YouTuber Trashbench以其创意十足、不拘一格的散热改装闻名。继此前利用露营冷却器对RTX 5050进行暴力超频后，他在最新视频中再次突破常规：将原本为大型工作站CPU设计的Arctic WS360 360毫米服务器一体式水冷散热器，直接改装到了GeForce RTX 3080显卡上。这场实验不仅造就了一个外观震撼的改装作品，更使显卡显存温度直接减半，并带来了显著的能效提升。

Arctic WS360的核心优势在于其超规格的冷头铜底，其面积是普通消费级水冷的两倍以上。正是这个巨大的尺寸，使其意外地非常适合覆盖现代GPU的核心及周围的GDDR6X显存芯片，几乎能完美嵌入显卡的电压调节模块（VRM）阵列之间。安装后，其外观与RTX 3080浑然一体，仿佛原厂设计。

然而，实现完美贴合的过程充满挑战。由于WS360的冷头完全遮蔽了显卡的标准安装孔位，Trashbench不得不通过反复设计和3D打印，经历了十多次试错，才制作出一套功能完备的自定义安装支架。尽管他在视频中以一贯的澳洲式随性风格展示，但其背后所需的工作量和耐心不容小觑。

改装完成后，散热效果在3DMark Time Spy测试中表现卓越。具体数据如下：GPU核心温度降低了26°C，核心热点温度降低了39°C，而显存温度更是从灼热的104°C骤降至冰冷的50°C，降幅达54°C。由于现代GPU对温度限制极为敏感，如此大幅的降温立刻转化为性能收益。即使在未手动超频的情况下，这台水冷RTX 3080的性能已与风冷版本在极限超频状态下的表现持平。更值得一提的是，水冷显卡在默认设置下的功耗低于风冷版，同时性能更优。

当Trashbench对水冷显卡施加手动超频时，核心频率得以再提升195 MHz。这使得游戏平均性能相比原厂风冷基线提升了约9%。在当前英伟达为显卡预留的超频空间已相当有限的情况下，这一提升已属显著，且无需进行风险更高的BIOS或分流器改装。

当然，如同所有极限改装，我们并不建议普通用户在家中尝试复现。Arctic WS360本身是昂贵的服务器硬件，且定制安装支架需要极大的耐心和反复调试。然而，这个项目精彩地揭示了：现代GPU在温度墙的背后，实际上隐藏着巨大的性能与能效潜力。通过突破散热瓶颈，即便是在现有硬件架构下，也能挖掘出可观的额外性能空间。

items.title: Minisforum AI X1 Pro 470 review: AMD's Gorgon Point in a sleek mini PC desktop
items.description: Minisforum’s AI X1 Pro 470 mini PC is just an incremental update to last year's well-regarded AI X1 Pro 370, but the barebones model isn't much more expensive.
items.content: Mini PCs have gained a following over recent years by bringing the best mobile silicon to the desktop. The mix of processing power, the compact chassis, power efficiency, and portability hits a PC sweet spot for many folks. Here we are looking at Hong Kong-based Minisforum’s update to its well-regarded AI X1 Pro 370 from last year – the new Minisforum AI X1 Pro 470. As given away by the name, this model packs one of the latest AMD Gorgon Point mobile processors.Specifically, the AMD Ryzen AI 9 HX 470 is installed in this machine, and the mini PC’s relative charms rely on the enhancements delivered by the AMD Ryzen AI 400 series. Sadly, the latest AMD mobile processors offer merely incremental uplifts, leaving enthusiasts underwhelmed. The CPU boost goes 100 MHz higher, and the NPU performance has been lifted from 50 to 55 TOPS in the latest silicon. Nevertheless, we thought it would be worthwhile to gauge the appeal of the new Minisforum AI X1 Pro 470, and check out the top-end AI 400 series laptop chip in a mini PC.Image 1 of 2(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)Design of the Minisforum AI X1 Pro 470Minisforum AI X1 Pro-470 at Amazon for $1,359The Minisforum AI X1 Pro 470 looks and feels well-made, hewn from premium materials, with nicely tactile buttons, and is reassuringly dense. An obvious comparison is against an Apple Mac Mini M4, but at 7.68 inches square and 1.87 inches tall, the Minisforum would be visibly larger than the Apple (5 x 5 x 2 inches), set side by side.If getting something Mac Mini-sized with an x86 chip is crucial, Minisforum offers the AI X1 (non-Pro) models, which almost exactly match the Mac’s dimensions. These non-Pro models from Minisforum are a bit cheaper, but they have fewer expansion options, ports, and a less potent cooling system.One of the design features I appreciate about the Minisforum AI X1 Pro 470 is that it has flexible positioning and mounting options straight from the box. It ships with both a metal VESA mount bracket and fixings, as well as a matching vertical stand. The larger ‘Pro’ models (our test model) also benefit from integrating a 135W PSU, so you only need a common figure-of-8 power lead (supplied) to the wall.Image 1 of 4(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)The Minisforum AI X1 Pro 470 has a decent selection of modern ports, mostly front and back. It is good to see OCuLink and 2x USB4 among them, and some will appreciate the dual 2.5G LAN ports at the back. I would have wished for more USB-A ports at the back and a rear audio jack, too.Other design features of note, which might make this stand out from a raft of rivals, are Minisforum’s integration of a Copilot button on the front of the chassis, its responsive Windows Hello-friendly fingerprint sensor on the top of the chassis, and the inclusion of stereo speakers and dual-array microphones under the hood.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsMinisforum AI X1 Pro 470 Mini PC SpecificationsSwipe to scroll horizontallyProcessorAMD Ryzen AI 9 HX 470 with 12C/24T, base/boost of 2.0/5.1 GHzGraphicsAMD Radeon 890M iGPU at up to 2.9 GHzRAM32GB DDR5-5600, up to 128GB (64GB x 2)Storage1TB PCIe4 NVMe (up to 12TB via 3x M.2 2280 slots)Physical portsOCuLink, 2x USB4, 2x USB 3.2 Gen 2, 1x USB 2.0 , HDMI 2.1, DP2.0, 2x 2.5G Ethernet, SD card slot, 3.5mm audio, mains power cableWirelessM.2 2230 E-Key (Wi-Fi 7 & Bluetooth 5.4)Other featuresFingerprint reader, Copilot button, stereo speakers, dual-array mic, VESA and vertical mounts suppliedDimensions195х195х42.5/47.5mm(LWH), 1.5kgOS installedWindows 11 ProPrice as configured$1,379 (bare bones is $779)Today's best Minisforum AI X1 Pro-470 deals $1,359View $1,359View $1,359ViewShow moreWe check over 250 million products every day for the best pricesPorts and Connectivity of the Minisforum AI X1 Pro 470While the AI X1 Pro 470 has a wide selection of ports, I’d like more. Having 2x USB 3.2 Gen 2 Type-A (10Gbps) ports, a USB4 Type-C (40 Gbps, supports Alt DisplayPort 2.0 & 15W PD out), and a 3.5mm Combo Audio Jack on the front, alongside the power and Copilot buttons, is a good start. To the left side, there is a full-sized SD card slot, with a Kensington Lock on the right.Round the back there are numerous of ports, including another USB4 Type-C (40 Gbps, supports 100W PD-in to power the PC, Alt DisplayPort 2.0, and 15W PD out), another USB Type-A (but USB 2.0), plus an OCuLink Port, HDMI 2.1 FRL, DisplayPort 2.0, 2x 2.5 Gigabit Ethernet (RJ45), and an AC inlet (next to a reset / clear CMOS hole).That sounds like a lot of variety, but just a single slow USB-A around the back is poor. It means I’ll be using a USB-C dock/dongle at the back, so the front system ports can remain ‘clean’ and available for handy occasional peripheral plugging. On the other hand, this little PC can connect up to four 4K screens simultaneously, straight from the box.Wireless connectivity is decent. A MediaTek MT7925 Wi-FI 7 Card fitted to the motherboard also provides Bluetooth 5.4 with BLE support.Upgradability of the Minisforum AI X1 Pro 470Before even opening up this device, it is pleasing to see that both OCuLink and USB4 are available for fast docking and eGPU connectivity. Inside, there’s quite a bit of room for upgrades, too – especially for a mini PC.Minisforum provides three M.2 storage slots. With my 32GB/1TB config, one of the slots already has a 1TB drive installed, specifically a Kingston PCIe Gen4 NVMe drive with HMB caching and QLC NAND. Adequate but nothing fancy, it managed peak read and write speeds of almost 5.7 GB/s and 5.0 GB/s, respectively, in ATTO Disk Benchmark. According to the specs, this mini PC can fit three such drives, maxing out at 12TB total capacity (3x 4TB). Extra fittings and thermal pads are supplied in the box.This ‘AI mini PC’ can pack up to 128GB of DDR5 RAM, using two SODIMM slots. You can buy it barebones direct from Minisforum or Amazon. We received a 32GB/1TB model for testing. These Ryzen AI 300 and 400 chips can be sensitive to memory bandwidth, especially for tasks that lean on the iGPU, so it was unfortunate that the 32GB here was a single DDR5-5600 module. In a ‘normal’ market, we think Minisforum would have supplied 2x 32GB, or a 2x 16GB option, but this 32GB was supplied with a single SODIMM, which seems to be a by-product of the RAMpocalypse.Image 1 of 3(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)Adding or changing RAM or storage requires removing five screws from the bottom of the device (1 short, 4 long), followed by 7 further internal screws marked with triangles. Then lift the PSU with the secondary cooling fan gently, as there are three wired connections to the motherboard. You don’t need to detach these cables; there’s enough clearance to lean this assembly to the side, get in, and reach the RAM and SSD slots.Productivity and AI Performance on the Minisforum AI X1 Pro 470We tested the Minisforum AI X1 Pro system, and its AMD Ryzen AI 9 HX 470 processor, using the supplied RAM/Storage configuration with Windows 11 pre-installed and the default ‘Balanced’ profile selected in the BIOS. VRAM was set at 8GB in the BIOS.Taken in isolation, the results look pretty good. However, as I had an Asus Zephyrus G16 with 32GB quad-channel DDR5-7500 RAM at hand (my personal desktop replacement portable), I thought it would be interesting to check the difference between the Ryzen AI 9 HX 370 (Asus laptop, PL1 65W, PL2 70W) and Ryzen AI 9 HX 470 (Minisforum mini PC, PL1 60W, PL2 65W).Due to the aforementioned mini PC RAM configuration, the older-gen, thermally-constrained Asus laptop trounced the new-gen Minisforum challenger. Disappointing. I wanted to see if the Minisforum could fight back if I upgraded it to dual-channel. Luckily, I could source a matching DDR5-5600 SO-DIMM, so I’ve also tabulated those results. Spoiler: the RAM upgrade makes a huge difference, convincingly putting the Gorgon Point processor in the mini PC into pole position most of the time.Swipe to scroll horizontallyRow 0 - Cell 0 X1 Pro 470 (1x 32GB)Zephyrus G16 HX 370 (32GB)X1 Pro 470 (2x 32GB) Cinebench R23 single / multi thread2,040 / 21,4761,895 / 21,0982,041 / 22,489Geekbench 6 single / multi thread2,858 / 11,3192,777 / 14,7152,927 / 14,652Proyon AI: Stable Diffusion NPU test2,7883,2883,602Handbrake 4K video transcode2m 56s2m 14s2m 14sAbove, you have two popular synthetic CPU benchmarks that concentrate on CPU performance. Then the Procyon AI tests I did looked at AI image generation using the onboard NPU. These results equate to a series of 500x500 pixel AI-generated images being produced from a prompt in Stable Diffusion Lite every 10 or 11s. Last but not least, Handbrake was used to convert a reference 4K/60 HDR nature video to 1080p/30.Gaming Performance on the Minisforum AI X1 Pro 470What we said about the single-vs-dual channel memory above applies even more in 3D gaming workloads that need to squeeze the best out of the Radeon 890M iGPU. Most readers will know about the theoretical benefits of improved memory bandwidth for Radeon iGPU graphics performance, but seeing, feeling, and measuring it is something else. We can thank the AI industry for opening our eyes to this and moving an extra matching memory module far beyond an impulse purchase.Swipe to scroll horizontallyRow 0 - Cell 0 X1 Pro 470 (1x 32GB)Zephyrus HX 370 (32GB)X1 Pro 470 (2x 32GB)3DMark Time Spy points2,3023,3684,1243DMark Night Raid points19,83930,17029,644Borderlands 3 1080p Medium fps29.4550.6361.80Shadow of the Tombraider 1080p Medium fps254448Far Cry 5 1080p normal fps284657Cyberpunk 2077 1080p ‘Steam Deck’ fps 27.2047.3550.89As I was thinking this Minisforum AI X1 Pro 470 would be a good candidate for a living room TV connected media box and 'Steam Machine,' I tested Cyberpunk 2077 using the ‘Steam Deck’ preset at 1080p. The system did pretty well after it was upgraded to dual-channel RAM.Overall, the gaming frame rates of 50-60fps on the dual-channel RAM-equipped X1 Pro 470 point to it being a pretty decent entertainment box for the living room scenario that I was thinking about. With all these tests done at medium settings (except CP2077), there are still plenty of settings that can be adjusted to achieve an average 60fps at 1080p, if you insist on that.Bottom LineDuring even the most demanding tests, the Minisforum AI X1 Pro 470 stayed pleasingly quiet and cool. Running through the benchmarks and stress tests, I didn’t see any processor temperatures rising above the low 70s (Celsius). Moreover, the weighty dual-fan cooling seems to do its job pretty quietly inside the slick metal alloy shell. These are definite advantages over my Zephyrus with Ryzen Ai 9 HX 370.An unobtrusive fan profile is important to me for long-term use of any PC as a daily driver. I think this system could make the grade, with its idle noise level of 28dBA and highest reading of 32dBA from approximately arms-length away (positioned beside my monitor on the desk). (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)I’ve enjoyed testing the Minisforum AI X1 Pro 470. It looks good, feels well built, offers flexible mounting and positioning options, and runs cool and quiet. Being able to check it out in dual-channel memory mode also raised my opinion of the device. A decent USB4 dock attached to the rear provides the ports I need, while keeping it tidy, too.Given the small changes delivered by AMD’s latest mobile chips, bargain hunters might be more interested in the AI X1 Pro 370 from last year, which is still available at $735 barebones, or $1,184 for the 32GB/1TB config. Also, if the AI X1 Pro 470 with 32GB/1TB config we received ($1,379.00 on Amazon) isn’t your little dream machine, the barebones model with HX 470 is just $779. To that, you'd need to add a dual-channel DDR5 SO-DIMM kit, an M.2 SSD, and Windows (or Linux).Minisforum AI X1 Pro-470: Price Comparison $1,359View $1,359View $1,359View $1,359ViewWe check over 250 million products every day for the best pricespowered by
items.author: Mark Tyson
items.titleZh: Minisforum AI X1 Pro 470评测：搭载AMD Gorgon Point的精美迷你PC机箱
items.descriptionZh:
近年来，迷你PC通过将顶尖的移动芯片技术引入桌面领域，赢得了特定用户群体的青睐。其在处理性能、紧凑机身、能效和便携性之间取得的平衡，满足了许多人对PC的“甜蜜点”需求。本文聚焦于总部位于香港的Minisforum对其去年广受好评的AI X1 Pro 370进行的升级——全新的Minisforum AI X1 Pro 470。正如其名所示，这款机型搭载了AMD最新的移动处理器系列之一：Gorgon Point，具体型号为AMD锐龙AI 9 HX 470。这款迷你PC的核心吸引力很大程度上依赖于AMD锐龙AI 400系列带来的增强特性。

然而，最新的AMD移动处理器提升幅度有限，仅属迭代升级，让部分爱好者略感失望。其CPU加速频率提升了100 MHz，NPU（神经网络处理单元）性能从上一代的50 TOPS提升至55 TOPS。尽管如此，评估新款Minisforum AI X1 Pro 470的吸引力，并探究这款顶级AI 400系列笔记本芯片在迷你PC中的表现，仍然具有重要价值。

**设计与规格**
Minisforum AI X1 Pro 470做工扎实，采用优质材料，按键触感良好，机身密度高，给人稳重之感。其尺寸为边长约7.68英寸（约195毫米）、高1.87英寸（约47.5毫米），与尺寸更紧凑的Apple Mac Mini M4（5 x 5 x 2英寸）相比明显更大。若用户追求x86芯片且尺寸接近Mac Mini的设备，Minisforum还提供尺寸几乎与Mac完全一致的AI X1（非Pro）型号，价格更便宜，但扩展选项、端口数量和散热系统效能相对较弱。

设计上的一个亮点是其灵活的定位和安装选项。包装内附带了金属VESA壁挂支架及配件，以及一个匹配的垂直立架。更大的“Pro”型号（即评测机型）还集成了135W电源适配器，用户仅需连接一根常见的8字电源线即可使用。

端口方面，该机型提供了较为丰富的现代接口。前面板包括2个USB 3.2 Gen 2 Type-A（10Gbps）、1个USB4 Type-C（40 Gbps，支持Alt DisplayPort 2.0和15W PD输出）、一个3.5mm复合音频插孔，以及电源键和Copilot专用按键。左侧设有一个全尺寸SD卡槽，右侧有 Kensington安全锁孔。后部接口包括：另一个USB4 Type-C（40 Gbps，支持100W PD输入供电、Alt DisplayPort 2.0和15W PD输出）、1个USB 2.0 Type-A、1个OCuLink端口、HDMI 2.1 FRL、DisplayPort 2.0、2个2.5千兆以太网口（RJ45）以及交流电源接口。这意味着它可以轻松直连最多四台4K显示器。后置仅有一个低速USB-A端口被认为是其不足。无线连接则由主板上的联发科MT7925 Wi-Fi 7网卡提供，同时支持蓝牙5.4和BLE。

其他值得注意的设计特点包括：机身前部的Copilot专用按键、顶部响应灵敏且支持Windows Hello的指纹传感器，以及内置的立体声扬声器和双阵列麦克风。

**内部结构与可升级性**
在不开机箱的情况下，OCuLink和USB4端口为快速扩展坞和外接显卡（eGPU）连接提供了可能。内部升级空间对于迷你PC而言相当可观。
*   **存储**：Minisforum提供了三个M.2 2280存储插槽。评测机型预装了一块1TB的金士顿PCIe Gen4 NVMe固态硬盘（采用HMB缓存和QLC NAND），实测峰值读写速度分别接近5.7 GB/s和5.0 GB/s。该迷你PC最多可安装三块此类硬盘，总容量最高可达12TB（3x 4TB）。包装内附带了额外的固定件和散热垫。
*   **内存**：这款“AI迷你PC”通过两个SODIMM插槽最高支持128GB DDR5内存。用户可以从Minisforum或亚马逊购买准系统版本（不含内存和存储）。值得注意的是，评测收到的32GB/1TB配置机型仅安装了一条DDR5-5600内存模块。由于AMD锐龙AI 300和400系列芯片，特别是其集成的Radeon显卡，对内存带宽较为敏感，单通道配置成为了一个性能瓶颈。在正常的市场环境下，厂商通常会提供双通道配置（2x16GB或2x32GB），但当前的单条32GB配置似乎是受“内存市场波动”影响的产物。

升级内存或存储需要卸下底部的5颗螺丝（1短4长），再移除内部标记有三角形的7颗螺丝。然后小心提起带有辅助冷却风扇的电源单元（PSU）部分（无需断开连接主板的三根线缆），即可接触到内存和SSD插槽。

**生产力与AI性能测试**
测试在预装Windows 11、BIOS设置为默认“平衡”模式、显存分配为8GB的条件下进行，使用了出厂的内存/存储配置（单条32GB DDR5-5600）。

为了进行对比，评测者还使用了一台搭载锐龙AI 9 HX 370处理器、配备32GB四通道DDR5-7500内存的华硕Zephyrus G16笔记本电脑作为参照。

**关键发现：双通道内存的巨大影响**
由于前述的单通道内存限制，搭载新一代HX 470处理器的Minisforum迷你PC在多项测试中，竟然不敌受散热限制的上一代HX 370笔记本，结果令人失望。为了探究性能潜力，评测者为其加装了另一条匹配的DDR5-5600内存，升级为双通道配置。

**性能对比数据摘要：**
*   **Cinebench R23（CPU渲染）**：单核成绩基本持平（~2040分），但双通道内存下的多核成绩（22489分）显著超越了单通道（21476分）和上代HX 370笔记本（21098分）。
*   **Geekbench 6（CPU综合）**：单核性能略有提升（2927 vs 2858），而双通道内存下的多核性能（14652分）大幅提升，几乎追平了上代笔记本（14715分）。
*   **Procyon AI（Stable Diffusion NPU测试）**：双通道配置下，NPU生成图像的速度（得分3602）明显快于单通道（2788），也超过了上代HX 370（3288），展示了NPU性能与内存带宽的关联。
*   **Handbrake（4K视频转码）**：双通道配置下，转码时间（2分14秒）与上代HX 370笔记本持平，且比单通道配置（2分56秒）快得多。

结论是：**将内存升级为双通道配置带来了巨大的性能差异，使得迷你PC中的Gorgon Point处理器在大多数情况下都确立了领先地位。**

**游戏性能测试**
在更加依赖Radeon 890M集成显卡性能的3D游戏测试中，双通道内存带来的优势更为显著。

**游戏帧率对比（1080p分辨率，中画质预设，除特别说明外）：**
*   **3DMark Time Spy（DX12）**：双通道（4124分）相比单通道（2302分）提升近80%，也大幅超越了上代HX 370笔记本（3368分）。
*   **实际游戏帧率（双通道 vs 单通道）**：
    *   《边境之地3》：61.80 fps vs 29.45 fps （提升超100%）
    *   《古墓丽影：暗影》：48 fps vs 25 fps
    *   《孤岛惊魂5》：57 fps vs 28 fps
    *   《赛博朋克2077》（“Steam Deck”预设）：50.89 fps vs 27.20 fps
在升级双通道内存后，X1 Pro 470在1080p中等画质下，多数游戏能达到50-60 fps的帧率，对于作为连接客厅电视的媒体中心或“Steam游戏机”设想来说，表现相当不错。

**散热、噪音与总结**
即使在最高负载测试中，Minisforum AI X1 Pro 470也能保持令人满意的低温和低噪音。处理器温度未超过70摄氏度出头，厚重的双风扇散热系统在金属合金外壳内安静地工作。与评测者使用的HX 370笔记本相比，这是一个显著优势。噪音方面，在距离约一臂远的桌面上，其待机噪音约为28分贝，最高负载时约为32分贝，对于需要长期作为主力机使用的用户而言非常重要。

**综合评价**
Minisforum AI X1 Pro 470外观精致、做工优良、运行凉爽安静，并提供灵活的安装选项。将其升级至双通道内存模式后，其性能表现令人刮目相看。通过后部连接一个合适的USB4扩展坞，可以很好地解决端口需求，保持桌面整洁。

考虑到AMD最新移动芯片的提升幅度有限，追求性价比的用户可能会对去年款的AI X1 Pro 370更感兴趣（准系统价735美元，32GB/1TB配置约1184美元）。而对于AI X1 Pro 470，如果评测的32GB/1TB配置（亚马逊售价1379美元）不符合期望，其准系统版本（仅779美元）提供了更大的自定义空间，用户可自行添加双通道DDR5内存套件、M.2 SSD和操作系统（Windows或Linux），以获取最佳性能。

items.titleZh: 将误差传播理论集成至FMEDA框架（罗伯特·博世有限公司）
items.descriptionZh:
德国罗伯特·博世有限公司（Robert Bosch GmbH）近日发表了一篇题为《量化FMEDA安全指标中的不确定性：一种用于增强ASIC验证的误差传播方法》的技术论文。该研究聚焦于汽车系统中专用集成电路（ASIC）功能安全验证的核心需求——获得准确可靠的安全指标。

**背景与挑战**
在汽车功能安全标准ISO 26262的框架下，故障模式、影响及诊断分析（FMEDA）是评估ASIC安全性的关键定量分析工具。它用于计算单点故障度量（SPFM）、潜在故障度量（LFM）等关键安全指标。传统FMEDA方法在计算这些指标时，通常将失效率、诊断覆盖率等输入参数视为固定精确值。然而在实际工程中，这些参数本身存在不确定性（如来自过程波动、测量误差或统计估算偏差）。忽略这种不确定性可能导致对最终安全指标过于自信或错误的评估，从而给安全关键型汽车电子系统带来潜在风险。

**核心原理与创新**
该论文提出的核心创新在于，将**误差传播理论**系统地集成到FMEDA框架中。其技术路径如下：
1.  **不确定性建模**：首先，识别并量化所有输入参数（如基础失效率、诊断覆盖率、共因失效因子）的不确定性，将其表征为概率分布而非单一数值。
2.  **蒙特卡洛模拟**：采用蒙特卡洛模拟方法，根据输入参数的概率分布进行大量随机采样。
3.  **误差传播计算**：对于每一次采样组合，运行完整的FMEDA计算流程，得到一组安全指标结果（SPFM、LFM等）。通过重复成千上万次模拟，最终得到安全指标的概率分布。
4.  **量化与输出**：分析输出结果分布，不仅报告指标的均值（最佳估计值），更重要的是提供其置信区间（例如，90%或99%置信度下的取值范围），从而明确量化了指标的不确定性范围。

**性能分析与对比**
研究通过一个汽车ASIC的案例进行了验证。结果表明：
*   传统确定性FMEDA计算出的安全指标是一个没有上下文的单点数值。
*   而新的概率性方法揭示，在考虑参数不确定性后，**关键安全指标的实际值可能在一个显著的范围内波动**（例如，SPFM的计算结果可能在一个10%-35%的相对误差带内变化）。这个范围直观地反映了当前分析结果的可靠程度。
*   对比显示，当输入参数不确定性较大时，传统方法得出的“达标”结论可能是脆弱的；而新方法提供的置信区间能更稳健地支持安全决策，例如明确识别出需要改进测试覆盖或降低参数不确定性的领域。

**技术影响与应用场景**
这项研究对汽车半导体及更广泛的功能安全领域具有重要影响：
*   **提升验证严谨性**：为安全工程师提供了更强大的工具，以科学地评估和沟通FMEDA结果中的信心水平，符合ISO 26262中关于“置信区间”分析的理念。
*   **优化资源分配**：通过识别对最终指标不确定性影响最大的输入参数，可以指导研发团队将资源集中用于减少那些关键参数的不确定性（如进行更充分的测试以提高诊断覆盖率的估算精度）。
*   **支持安全决策**：在系统集成和硬件安全评估中，基于置信区间的指标有助于做出更知情、更可靠的安全合规判断，避免因隐藏的不确定性而导致的安全隐患。
*   **适用范围**：该方法虽然以汽车ASIC为例，但同样适用于任何需要执行定量FMEDA的领域，如工业自动化、航空航天和医疗器械中的安全关键型电子系统。

总之，博世的这项研究通过将误差传播理论引入FMEDA，推动了功能安全量化分析从“确定性”向“概率性”的演进，为在复杂半导体设计中实现更真实、更可靠的安全验证提供了重要的方法论进步。该论文已发表在专业媒体《半导体工程》上。

items.titleZh: 多GPU环境下LLM推理中CPU引发性能下降的系统性分析（佐治亚理工学院）
items.descriptionZh:
佐治亚理工学院近期发表的技术论文《多GPU LLM推理中CPU引发的减速现象特征分析》揭示了一个关键但常被忽视的系统瓶颈：在多GPU环境中运行大语言模型推理和服务时，CPU可能成为限制整体性能的主要因素。

**背景与问题**
随着大语言模型规模不断扩大，多GPU系统已成为支撑其推理与服务的主流硬件平台。研究团队发现，尽管计算负载主要分布在多个GPU上，但CPU在协调GPU工作、管理内存和数据传输等任务中扮演着核心角色。传统性能分析往往聚焦于GPU利用率，而CPU的潜在瓶颈则被系统性低估。

**核心技术原理与发现**
论文通过细致的实证分析，明确了CPU引发性能下降的两大主因：
1.  **内存分配与调度开销**：在多GPU流水线并行或动态批处理场景中，CPU需频繁为不同GPU分配和调度输入/输出数据。当请求并发量高或序列长度差异大时，CPU的调度与内存管理开销急剧上升，导致GPU等待数据，利用率下降。
2.  **通信与协调瓶颈**：GPU间的梯度同步、中间结果收集等操作需CPU参与协调。若CPU核心数不足或指令效率低下，会成为通信流程的阻塞点。

研究的关键创新在于，首次通过详尽的端到端性能剖析，量化了CPU活动（如上下文切换、内存分配调用）与GPU空闲时间之间的直接关联，并建立了可观测的系统指标（如CPU特定核心的饱和度）来预警此类性能瓶颈。

**性能数据与对比**
实验在配备多个现代GPU（如H100/A100）的服务器上进行，运行典型LLM（如GPT-3架构模型）。数据显示：
- 在高峰值请求负载下，由于CPU成为瓶颈，GPU利用率可下降高达40%。
- 即使GPU算力未饱和，系统吞吐量也可能因CPU限制而提前达到平台期。
- 对比不同CPU型号（如主流服务器CPU与更高频或更多核心的型号）发现，CPU单核性能与核心数量对多GPU推理的尾部延迟（P99延迟）有显著影响。

**技术影响**
这项研究对AI硬件与系统设计具有重要启示：
1.  **系统平衡设计**：在选择或配置多GPU服务器时，需避免“重GPU、轻CPU”的配置策略，应确保CPU拥有足够的核心数与高速缓存。
2.  **软件优化方向**：推理服务框架（如vLLM、Triton）可针对CPU调度器、内存分配器进行深度优化，减少GPU空闲等待。
3.  **性能评估方法**：为多GPU推理系统建立性能模型时，必须将CPU作为关键变量纳入考量。

**应用场景**
该分析直接影响所有依赖多GPU进行大规模LLM部署的场景：
- **AI云服务与API提供商**：需优化其服务器CPU配置与任务调度策略，以保障高并发下的服务稳定性与成本效益。
- **高性能计算中心**：在部署用于科学发现的LLM时，需进行CPU-GPU平衡性验证，避免资源浪费。
- **边缘AI盒子与一体机**：在资源受限的集成设备中，CPU瓶颈可能更为突出，设计时需格外关注。

总之，这项研究将业界目光引向了多GPU算力系统中这个“沉默的瓶颈”，为构建下一代高效、平衡的AI计算基础设施提供了重要的理论基础与优化方向。

history.title: $500 fiber optic HDMI cable delivers flawless 48 Gbps performance across a staggering 990 feet — crushes 8K at 60 Hz and 4K at 120 Hz over long distances
history.description: The HDMI standard can often be unclear, with the same version number referring to completely different capabilities across products. You can technically pay your way out of this confusion by going with an expensive fiber-optic HDMI cable that maintains its full bandwidth over much longer distances. That's what this Twitter user did, and their cable has one more trick up its sleeve.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Tom's Hardware Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware Newsletter Get Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox. Contact me with news and offers from other Future brands Receive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors By submitting your information you agree to the Terms & Conditions and Privacy Policy and are aged 16 or over. You are now subscribed Your newsletter sign-up was successful An account already exists for this email address, please log in. Subscribe to our newsletter Ultra-expensive cables positioned as a miracle cure have been around forever. Most of these are obviously shams, but some are actually really worth the price, such as those that use fiber optics. Cables ditching copper wires to improve signal integrity are not exactly rare, but they were a new discovery for this Twitter user, who got their hands on an $116 fiber-optic HDMI cable from Ruipro with detachable ends.Go deeper with TH Premium: GPUs (Image credit: Noctua)Desktop RoadmapEnterprise RoadmapRubin in-depthThe Stout Owl: The ultimate Noctua G2 PCThe X post below went viral, garnering almost a million views for what seems like a perfectly ordinary product. Be that as it may, most people don’t actually care for, or even know, tech specs off the top of their head, especially when it comes to confusing standards like HDMI. What we’re looking at here is an active optical cable (AOC), which carries its signal over fiber optics instead of traditional copper wiring.It’s called “active” because there’s a signal conversion taking place inside the cable, where electrical HDMI signals are converted into optical signals between the two ends. The cable isn’t entirely devoid of copper wires; some are still used for low-priority communication and power, but the majority of the signal is carried via fiber optic strands. This allows for significantly longer cables that can travel long distances without degradation.Article continues below You may like Start-up plans to use terahertz radio frequencies for communication between servers instead of copper or optical connections Brit installs adapters to convert telephone lines into gigabit ethernet carrier throughout his house Optical device beams data at speeds up to 25 Gbps via light, up to 25 kilometer range with ultra-low latency Look at this HDMI cable I just got 👀This isn’t a regular HDMI cable, it’s fiber optic. The signal is transmitted with light instead of copper, so there’s basically zero signal loss even over long distances.It’s HDMI 2.1, so it supports everything you’d want: 8K60, 4K120,… pic.twitter.com/T5ZnQOrf3CMarch 26, 2026Because this is an HDMI 2.1 cable in the post, it's capable of 8K output at 60 Hz, or 4K at 120 Hz without DSC, and supports 10-bit HDR. It's a full-fat 48 Gbps cable, which isn't always guaranteed, given how loose the HDMI Forum is with its branding. In fact, this is why people often buy overpriced cables: to circumvent the marketing red tape and get the best possible option that'll cover all their needs.Now, these specs aren't special in a vacuum, but the fact that the cable can enable them over (up to) 990 feet — that's the impressive bit. The "entry-level" $116 version is only 3 feet long, and for that, it's quite expensive because you don't need fiber optic for this length. The best deal here is probably the 100-foot cable priced at $150, so only about $30 more for an extra 97 feet of fiber-optic goodness.Ruipro has made the HDMI connectors on both ends removable, so you won't have to replace the entire cable if a plug breaks. When removed, the end of the cable can slot into keystone jacks and wall plates as well for easy storage. The cable itself is relatively thin for its size, and the connectors are made entirely of metal to ensure durability.Another benefit of fiber optic is its resistance to electromagnetic interference, though that's not a huge issue to begin with for HDMI, and EMI is notoriously used as the bait to sell those aforementioned miracle cures. Regardless, this is still a solid HDMI 2.1 cable for those who value signal integrity, and even though the starting price is certainly not enticing, the subsequent options are priced rather fairly.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.
history.author: Hassam Nasir
history.titleZh: 500美元光纤HDMI线材实现990英尺无损48Gbps传输——轻松胜任远距离8K 60Hz与4K 120Hz信号传输
history.descriptionZh:
近期，一条关于光纤HDMI线缆的推文引发了近百万次的观看，使得这类相对小众的高端线材进入大众视野。推文中展示的是Ruipro品牌一款售价116美元、端头可拆卸的光纤HDMI线缆。虽然市场上摒弃铜线以提升信号完整性的线缆并不罕见，但许多人对此类产品的具体技术原理和优势仍不甚了解，尤其是面对HDMI这类复杂的标准时。

这条线缆本质上是一种**有源光缆**（Active Optical Cable, AOC）。其核心创新在于，它并非完全使用铜线传输信号，而是在线缆内部进行了**光电转换**：输入端的电信号（HDMI信号）被转换为光信号，通过光纤进行传输，到达输出端后再转换回电信号。线缆内仍保留少量铜线，用于低优先级通信和供电，但主干信号通过光纤传输。这种设计带来了革命性的优势：**信号在极长距离内传输也不会衰减**。

性能方面，文中提到的线缆符合**HDMI 2.1标准**，提供完整的**48 Gbps带宽**，支持无需DSC（显示流压缩）的**8K@60Hz或4K@120Hz**输出，并兼容10位HDR。这些规格本身在高端铜缆上也能实现，但该光纤线缆的惊人之处在于其**传输距离可达990英尺（约302米）**，而同等长度的铜缆几乎无法保证无损传输。

价格策略上，入门级的3英尺（约0.9米）版本售价116美元，对于短距离应用而言性价比不高，因为铜缆完全足以胜任。但其长距离版本则显得颇具竞争力，例如100英尺（约30.5米）版本仅售150美元，仅比短款贵约34美元，却提供了近百英尺的光纤传输能力。

产品设计也有亮点：**两端的HDMI接头是可拆卸的**。这种设计不仅方便更换损坏的插头，还能将线缆端头插入墙面面板或配线架的Keystone模块中，便于理线和隐藏布线。线缆本体相对纤细，接头采用全金属材质以保证耐用性。

此外，光纤传输天生具备**抗电磁干扰（EMI）** 的能力，尽管对于HDMI应用环境而言，EMI通常不是主要问题，且常被一些“玄学”线材营销过度夸大。

总结来说，Ruipro的这款有源光纤HDMI线缆是为特定需求场景设计的专业解决方案：**它主要面向需要超长距离（例如超过15-20米）、且必须保证无损传输超高分辨率和高刷新率信号的专业影音安装、大型会场、数据中心或高端家庭影院项目**。对于普通用户的标准家用距离（1-3米），高端铜缆仍是更经济务实的选择。这款产品揭示了在高端影音和专业领域，通过光电转换技术突破铜缆物理限制的可行路径。

history.title: Intel Arrow Lake Refresh CPU prices shot up to 17% above MSRP just 48 hours after launch — dynamic pricing trumps manufacturer's recommended pricing
history.description: The new Core Ultra 200S Plus series has received widespread acclaim within the community, and we liked it as well for rejuvenating Intel's otherwise underwhelming CPU generation. Part of the reason they're getting good reviews is their excellent price-to-performance ratio, which seems to be slightly undone at the moment.

Subscribe to our newsletter Intel announced its Arrow Lake Refresh series earlier this month, with a March 26, 2026, launch for the Core Ultra 5 250K Plus and Core Ultra 7 270K Plus. As of this writing, it's been just two days since these CPUs were released, yet they're already selling above MSRP across retailers. The Core Ultra 7 270K Plus and the Core Ultra 5 250K Plus currently sit at around $349.99 and $219.99, respectively. Meanwhile, the iGPU-less Core Ultra 5 250KF Plus sells for $199.99.Check out the Core Ultra 5 250KF Plus on NeweggCheck out the Core Ultra 5 250K Plus on NeweggCheck out the Core Ultra 7 270K Plus on NeweggThe MSRP for the Core Ultra 7 270K Plus is $299, which means it's $50 more expensive than its list price on Newegg at the moment. We were able to find it for a bit less on B&H, but it's out of stock there. The Core Ultra 5 250K Plus was announced at $200, but it's selling for $220, while its iGPU-less counterpart, the Core Ultra 5 250KF Plus, is listed at $200, up $15 from its original ask of $185.Those prices are consistent across vendors, so getting a Core Ultra 200S series CPU at MSRP — even if the difference is minor — might not be possible right now. We're going through an unprecedented economic climate as the entire PC industry is stuck in limbo, navigating both geopolitical chokepoints and the AI boom's unending appetite for production lines.It's a borderline miracle we're even getting new value-oriented chips in this climate. We called the 250K Plus the best CPU for $200 in our review and gave it an Editor's Choice award. The 270K Plus is also a big improvement over its predecessor, but it falls behind when in gaming compared to X3D chips. Moreover, no matter how impressive these chips might be, the LGA 1851 socket is unfortunately a dead end.That might put some people off buying the Arrow Lake refresh, since Nova Lake is supposed to be a truly generational leap for Intel, perhaps even catapulting them ahead of AMD after years of stagnation. If you don't plan on upgrading and just want a good CPU now, the 250K Plus for $220 is a great deal, while the 270K Plus for $350 is another solid option for flagship performance without spending $500 on the 285K. history.author: Hassam Nasir
history.titleZh: 英特尔箭湖Refresh版CPU发布仅48小时后价格飙升至厂商建议零售价17%以上——动态定价机制凌驾于厂商指导价之上
history.descriptionZh:
英特尔于本月早些时候宣布了其Arrow Lake Refresh系列处理器，其中Core Ultra 5 250K Plus和Core Ultra 7 270K Plus已于2026年3月26日上市。令人关注的是，尽管发布仅两天，这两款CPU已在各大零售商处普遍出现溢价销售现象。

目前，Core Ultra 7 270K Plus的市场价格约为349.99美元，Core Ultra 5 250K Plus约为219.99美元。而无集成显卡的型号Core Ultra 5 250KF Plus售价为199.99美元。这与英特尔的官方建议零售价存在明显差距：Core Ultra 7 270K Plus的MSRP为299美元（现溢价约50美元），Core Ultra 5 250K Plus的MSRP为200美元（现溢价20美元），而Core Ultra 5 250KF Plus的MSRP为185美元（现溢价15美元）。该溢价趋势在各大供应商中表现一致，这意味着目前几乎无法以官方建议零售价购得Core Ultra 200S系列处理器，即便差价并不巨大。

这一市场状况发生在特殊的宏观经济背景下。整个PC产业正处在一个前所未有的、近乎停滞的“模糊期”，同时面临地缘政治导致的供应链瓶颈，以及AI热潮对半导体产能的持续争夺。在此环境下，英特尔仍能推出面向主流市场的换代芯片，实属不易。

从产品性能来看，Tom‘s Hardware在评测中将Core Ultra 5 250K Plus评为“200美元价位最佳CPU”并授予“编辑选择奖”。Core Ultra 7 270K Plus相比前代产品亦有显著提升，但在游戏性能方面仍落后于AMD的3D V-Cache（X3D）系列芯片。然而，一个重要的制约因素是，这些芯片所采用的LGA 1851插座平台已被确认是“终点站”，下一代Nova Lake架构将更换接口。

这可能会让部分消费者对购买Arrow Lake Refresh系列犹豫不决，因为预计Nova Lake将是英特尔一次真正的代际飞跃，甚至可能帮助其在多年的僵持后实现对AMD的领先。不过，如果用户并无近期升级计划，只是当前需要一款性能出色的CPU，那么220美元的Core Ultra 5 250K Plus性价比突出，而350美元的Core Ultra 7 270K Plus则为不愿花费500美元购买顶级型号Core Ultra 9 285K的用户提供了一个坚实的旗舰性能选择。

总而言之，Arrow Lake Refresh系列在严峻的市场环境中展现了其产品竞争力，但初期的溢价和即将换代的平台限制，是消费者在购买决策时需要权衡的关键因素。

history.title: Radeon RX 9070 XT bundle bonanza includes free SSDs, AIO coolers, and power supplies for $699 — Red Team rescues gamers with reasonably-priced GPU bundles
history.description: AMD’s RX 9070 XT drops to $699 with bundled extras, offering better overall value in a volatile market.

Subscribe to our newsletter The AMD Radeon RX 9070 XT is one of the best graphics cards in 2026. The graphics card is now available at $699 through different bundles. Newegg has listed the ASRock Challenger Radeon RX 9070 XT at $729.99, the Gigabyte Radeon RX 9070 XT Gaming OC at $739.99, and the Sapphire Pulse Radeon RX 9070 XT at $769.99. By applying dedicated promo codes at checkout, buyers can bring the price down to $699, plus a free copy of Crimson Desert and an additional PC component, depending on the model.Check out the Sapphire Pulse Radeon RX 9070 XT deal on NeweggCheck out the ASRock Challenger Radeon RX 9070 XT deal on NeweggCheck out the Gigabyte Radeon RX 9070 XT Gaming OC deal on NeweggThe ASRock Challenger Radeon RX 9070 XT includes a 360mm AIO liquid cooler, the Gigabyte Radeon RX 9070 XT Gaming OC comes with a free 512GB PCIe 4.0 SSD, and the Sapphire Pulse Radeon RX 9070 XT bundles a 750W Gold-rated power supply.The Radeon RX 9070 XT is a well-rounded 4K gaming GPU that offers performance on par with the RTX 5070 Ti at a lower cost. That said, the GPU market continues to spiral, as street prices for most modern GPUs are significantly higher than MSRP due to ongoing DRAM shortages driven by AI. On top of that, AMD recently raised the price of its Radeon RX 9000 series by $10 per 8GB of VRAM, effectively increasing the MSRP of the RX 9070 XT from $599 to $619.The Radeon RX 9070 XT was introduced last year, based on AMD’s RDNA 4 architecture, with 64 compute units, a boost clock speed of 2.97 GHz, 16GB of GDDR6 VRAM with 20 Gbps transfer speeds over a 256-bit interface, and a TBP (Total Board Power) of 304W. Our testing found that the RX 9070 XT delivers similar performance in both 1440p and 4K gaming at ultra settings compared to Nvidia’s RTX 5070 Ti. It is worth noting that the 5070 Ti is currently priced significantly higher, with prices starting at $1,000 compared to its MSRP of $749.For those looking to upgrade, these deals make the RX 9070 XT a more compelling option, especially with the free bundled items on Newegg. However, with prices still elevated across the board, buyers will need to weigh whether to jump in now or wait for the market to stabilize. history.titleZh: AMD RX 9070 XT显卡礼包狂潮：699美元即享免费固态硬盘、一体水冷和电源套装——红队以高性价比组合助力玩家
history.descriptionZh:
AMD Radeon RX 9070 XT 作为2026年顶尖显卡之一，现已通过多种捆绑套装上市，起售价为699美元。Newegg平台列出了多款非公版型号的具体售价：华擎 Challenger Radeon RX 9070 XT 售价729.99美元，技嘉 Radeon RX 9070 XT Gaming OC 售价739.99美元，蓝宝石 Pulse Radeon RX 9070 XT 售价769.99美元。消费者在结账时使用专属促销码，即可将价格降至699美元，并额外获赠《赤色沙漠》游戏副本以及一项随型号不同的PC组件——华擎型号捆绑360mm一体式水冷散热器，技嘉型号附赠512GB PCIe 4.0固态硬盘，蓝宝石型号则搭配750W金牌认证电源。

该显卡基于AMD RDNA 4架构打造，拥有64个计算单元，加速频率达2.97 GHz，配备16GB GDDR6显存，通过256位接口实现20 Gbps的传输速率，整板功耗（TBP）为304W。在性能表现上，RX 9070 XT 在1440p和4K分辨率下开启超高画质设置时，性能与英伟达 RTX 5070 Ti 持平，但当前市场售价显著低于对手。值得注意的是，RTX 5070 Ti 建议零售价为749美元，实际市价已飙升至1000美元起步，而RX 9070 XT 在捆绑优惠后性价比尤为突出。

然而，受人工智能热潮导致的DRAM持续短缺影响，整个GPU市场价格依然高企，多数现代显卡的实际售价远高于官方建议零售价。AMD近期亦实施显存关联调价策略，每8GB显存价格上涨10美元，致使RX 9070 XT 建议零售价从599美元上调至619美元。在当前市场环境下，有意升级显卡的用户需权衡：是趁捆绑优惠入手RX 9070 XT，还是等待市场价格趋于稳定。对于追求4K游戏体验且注重性价比的玩家而言，此次促销使RX 9070 XT 成为极具吸引力的选择。

history.title: Samsung preps PCIe 5.0 QLC SSD with a controller based on open-source RISC-V architecture — BM9K1 delivers speeds up to 11.4 GB/s for 'personal AI workloads'
history.description: The BM9K1 reportedly delivers sequential read speeds of up to 11.4 GB/s, which Samsung said is 1.6 times faster than its predecessor, the PCIe 4.0 BM9C1.

Subscribe to our newsletter As reported by BigGo Finance, Samsung has unveiled the company's next-generation BM9K1 SSD at the China Flash Market Summit 2026 (CFMS 2026). The BM9K1, which will rival the best SSDs, is a PCIe 5.0 drive with QLC NAND and features an SSD controller built around the open-source RISC-V architecture.Go deeper with TH Premium: AI shortages (Image credit: Nvidia)AI data centers are swallowing the world's memory and storage supplyChip scarcity assaults auto industry amid the worsening Nexperia and DRAM crisisSamsung and SK hynix shorten memory contracts as pricing power shifts back to suppliersMemory makers are set to earn $551 billion from the AI boomThe BM9K1 reportedly delivers sequential read speeds of up to 11.4 GB/s, which Samsung said is 1.6 times faster than its predecessor, the PCIe 4.0 BM9C1. Samsung is targeting a 2027 launch in 512GB, 1TB, and 2TB capacities and positioning the drive for what the company calls “personal AI computing” workloads on desktops and laptops.Notable with the BM9K1 is Samsung’s departure from an Arm-based controller to a proprietary one built in-house using the open-source RISC-V instruction set architecture. The company said the RISC-V design enables more granular firmware optimization for QLC NAND management and AI-specific I/O patterns, resulting in a 23% improvement in energy efficiency over the BM9C1. That gain in efficiency is naturally going to make a big difference in thermally constrained, small-form-factor applications focused on AI.Article continues below You may like Micron announces 3610 SSD, the industry-first PCIe 5.0 QLC SSD available to OEMs Chinese SSD maker YMTC lists its first commercial PCIe 5.0 SSD as worldwide shortage intensifies Phison's new SSD controller sips a mere 2.3W and runs at 14.7 GB/s, addressing PCIe 5.0's power concerns Samsung BM9K1 SSD SpecificationsSwipe to scroll horizontallyRow 0 - Cell 0 Samsung BM9K1Samsung BM9C1Micron 3610Samsung 9100 ProInterfacePCIe 5.0 (NVMe)PCIe 4.0 (NVMe)PCIe 5.0 (NVMe 2.0)2.0)PCIe 5.0 (NVMe)NANDQLCQLCQLC (G9 276-layer)TLC (V8)Seq. read11.4 GB/s5 GB/s11 GB/s14.8 GB/sSeq. writeNot disclosed4.5 GB/s9.3 GB/s13.4 GB/sControllerSamsung RISC-VSamsung 5nm-Samsung 5nm PrestoCapacities512GB, 1TB, 2TB2TB512GB, 1TB, 2TB1TB, 2TB, 4TB512GB, 1TB, 2TB, 4TB, 8TBWith its introduction, the BM9K1 also enters a small category of similar drives. Micron’s 3610, announced at CES back in January, is the only other PCIe 5.0 QLC client SSD announced so far. The 3610 reaches 11 GB/s sequential reads and 9.3 GB/s writes, and Micron is already sampling it to OEM partners in capacities up to 4TB. Samsung's drive edges ahead in raw sequential read throughput at 11.4 GB/s, but tops out at 2TB and has not disclosed write speeds.Both QLC drives sit well below Samsung's own TLC-based 9100 Pro and its OEM counterpart, the PM9E1, which delivers up to 14.8 GB/s of read speed using V8 TLC NAND and the 5nm Presto controller. There’s a cost trade-off, though, with QLC packing four bits per cell versus TLC's three, yielding higher density and lower per-gigabyte pricing at the expense of write endurance and sustained write performance.Samsung plans to bring the BM9K1 to market in 2027, but hasn’t disclosed any information on pricing or form factor. history.author: Luke James
history.titleZh: 三星发布采用开源RISC-V架构控制器的PCIe 5.0 QLC固态硬盘——BM9K1型号为个人AI工作负载提供高达11.4 GB/s传输速度
history.descriptionZh:
在2026年中国闪存市场峰会（CFMS 2026）上，三星电子正式发布了其下一代消费级固态硬盘BM9K1。该产品旨在与市场上顶级SSD竞争，是一款采用PCIe 5.0接口和QLC NAND闪存的驱动器，其最大亮点在于搭载了基于开源RISC-V架构自主研发的SSD主控芯片。

**背景与产品定位**
随着AI数据中心消耗全球大量存储资源，以及芯片短缺持续影响多个行业，存储市场正经历快速变革。三星将BM9K1定位于满足台式机和笔记本电脑的“个人AI计算”工作负载，计划于2027年上市，提供512GB、1TB和2TB三种容量选择。

**核心技术原理与创新点**
BM9K1的核心创新在于其控制器架构的转变。三星放弃了此前采用的Arm架构，转向基于开源RISC-V指令集架构自研主控。这一转变使得三星能够进行更精细的固件优化，专门针对QLC NAND的管理和AI特有的I/O访问模式进行定制。QLC（四层单元）NAND通过在单个存储单元中存放4比特数据，实现了比传统TLC（三层单元）更高的存储密度和更低的每GB成本，但在写入寿命和持续写入性能上存在折衷。新的RISC-V控制器通过对QLC的优化管理，在能效上取得了显著提升。

**性能参数与对比分析**
据三星披露，BM9K1的顺序读取速度最高可达11.4 GB/s，这比其前代PCIe 4.0产品BM9C1（5 GB/s）快了1.6倍。能效方面，得益于RISC-V架构的优化，BM9K1比BM9C1提升了23%，这对于注重散热和功耗的紧凑型AI计算设备至关重要。

目前，市面上已宣布的PCIe 5.0 QLC消费级SSD仅有美光在年初CES上发布的3610型号。根据提供的对比表格，美光3610的顺序读写速度分别为11 GB/s和9.3 GB/s，且已向OEM伙伴提供最高4TB容量的样品。三星BM9K1在顺序读取速度上以11.4 GB/s略微领先，但最大容量为2TB，且未公布写入速度。

两款QLC产品的性能均显著低于三星自家采用TLC NAND和5nm Presto控制器的旗舰产品9100 Pro（及其OEM型号PM9E1），后者的读取速度高达14.8 GB/s。这清晰体现了QLC与TLC之间的性能/成本权衡：QLC以牺牲部分写入性能和耐用性为代价，换取了更高的存储密度和更低的单位成本。

**技术影响与应用场景**
BM9K1的发布标志着RISC-V架构在关键存储组件中实现了重要的商业化落地。其更高的能效优化特别适合应用于对热设计功耗（TDP）敏感的轻薄笔记本电脑、小型台式机以及专注于本地AI推理和内容创作的“个人AI计算”场景。在AI工作负载日益从云端向边缘设备扩散的趋势下，此类高性能、高能效的存储解决方案将变得愈发重要。

**总结**
三星BM9K1 SSD通过采用创新的自研RISC-V控制器和QLC NAND，在PCIe 5.0平台上实现了显著的性能提升与能效进步。尽管在绝对性能上与传统TLC旗舰产品有差距，且初期容量选择有限，但其针对AI工作负载和能效的优化设计，预示着存储产品正朝着更专业化、更适应边缘计算需求的方向发展。该产品预计2027年上市，具体定价和物理尺寸（如是否为标准M.2 2280）尚未公布。

history.title: Meta to fund seven new natural gas power plants to fuel AI data centers — Entergy partnership to deliver 7 gigawatts of power for Louisiana AI facility
history.description: Meta is paying for the construction of seven new natural gas plants to supply its largest data center, massively expanding the fossil fuel infrastructure underpinning its AI buildout.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 5 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Tom's Hardware Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware Newsletter Get Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.
